title: Boat Rental in Amazon Manaus: Costs, Tips and Seasons
html:
Boat rental in Amazon Manaus puts the meeting of the waters, river beaches and igarapés within reach, with most trips leaving from Marina do Davi and day prices typically running from EUR 220 to EUR 1,400 in 2026. BoatHire24 lists crewed and self-drive options across the region.

Where to set off in Manaus

The city sits where the dark Río Negro meets the pale Solimões, and most boats leave from a handful of well known points. Each departure suits a different kind of day, from quick river runs to full expeditions upriver.

  • Marina do Davi: the most popular launch point on the Río Negro, busy with day boats.
  • Porto de Manaus: the central waterfront by the historic customs house.
  • Encontro das Águas: the meeting of the waters, around 13 nautical miles downstream from the centre.
  • Anavilhanas: a vast river archipelago up the Río Negro, reached on longer crewed trips.
  • Praia da Lua: a soft river beach close to Manaus, popular in the dry season.

Which boat to rent in Manaus

The right boat depends on how far you travel and how many days you have. For the Encontro das Águas a fast motorboat is ideal, while trips into Anavilhanas suit a regional riverboat with hammocks or cabins.

  1. Regional riverboat: best for overnight expeditions and hammock travel, seats 8 to 20 guests, from EUR 300 per day.
  2. Covered voadeira: a small aluminium speedboat for quick tours, carries 4 to 8 guests, from EUR 180 per day.
  3. Motor yacht: best for a comfortable, fast day, the Fairline Targa 43 type carries up to 10 guests, from EUR 900 per day.
  4. Crewed cruiser: best for several nights upriver, sleeps 6 to 12 guests, from EUR 700 per day.

Is a guide needed to explore the Amazon by boat?

Yes, a local guide or crew is strongly advised in Manaus, because channels, currents and flooded forest change with the season. The basin is enormous and unmarked, and short hops cross open water where the Río Negro is several kilometres wide. A skipper who knows the igarapés will find dolphins, beaches and quiet creeks you would otherwise miss. For a faster, sportier day, the Fairline Targa 43 listing shows the kind of motor yacht that pairs a crew with comfort.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Manaus?

Prices in Manaus depend on the boat, the crew and how far upriver you go. Shared tours are cheap while private cruisers cost the most, and the live rates for boat hire Amazon Manaus let you weigh each option. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Manaus or list your own.

  • A shared meeting of the waters tour starts from about EUR 35 per person.
  • A small motorboat for a private half day runs from roughly EUR 180.
  • A covered day boat or pontoon for a full day costs from around EUR 350.
  • A crewed yacht for a full day starts near EUR 900 and rises with cabins and distance.

When is the best season to rent a boat in Manaus?

The dry season from June to November is the prime window, when river beaches appear and the water is calmer. From December to May the high water season floods the forest, ideal for paddling among the trees but covering the sandy beaches. The Río Negro rarely sees strong sea winds, though afternoon squalls and the local friagem cold snap blow in from the south.

Plan the meeting of the waters early in the morning: the Río Negro and Solimões run side by side for several kilometres before mixing, and the contrast shows best in calm light before midday heat builds.

On paperwork, Brazil requires registered vessels and a qualified skipper for charter. Do not assume a foreign licence transfers, and always let the operator confirm the exact requirements for your boat and route before you pay.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 35 per person shared to EUR 1,400 per day private
  • Peak months: June to November dry season
  • Trip length: 4 hours to several nights
  • Capacity: 4 to 20 guests by boat
  • Currency: EUR on BoatHire24 listings
  • Signature trip: Encontro das Águas, around 13 nautical miles out

Questions about boat rental in Manaus

Can you see the meeting of the waters on a boat trip?

Yes, nearly every Manaus day trip includes the Encontro das Águas, where the dark Río Negro and sandy Solimões flow side by side for kilometres before blending, around 13 nautical miles downstream.

Are pink river dolphins easy to spot?

Sightings are common but never guaranteed, since the botos are wild animals; trips toward Novo Airão and the Anavilhanas archipelago offer the best chances, especially in the early morning.

How many days does an Amazon boat trip need?

A single day covers the meeting of the waters and a forest creek, while reaching Anavilhanas comfortably needs two to four days, giving time for dolphins, beaches and quiet igarapés upriver.

Booking ahead secures the better crews during the busy dry months, so compare boats, read the inclusions and reserve early for the best of Manaus on the water. Boat owners across the Amazon can also list their vessels with BoatHire24 to reach travellers worldwide.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Manaus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
